evidence_quotes.quote: Games you bought with your money can be completely down after EOL

paragraphs.text: I believe that the current landscape of the video game industry is hostile to the customer. Games you bought with your money can be completely down after EOL and it will stay that way forever unless we have a way to prevent this. This makes me scared because I play video games as a hobby, and games that I wanted to play are now no longer playable which makes me wish that I had a time machine. This isn't an impossible issue, as it has been proven that publishers CAN make their games playable after EOL, they simply don't allocate the resources to do so. Resources that I might add is just a simple plan to incorporate into the development lifecycle a way to let the customer play the game after EOL.
